name: Circular El Barrancazo y Paterna del Madera
name_original: Peñascosa y Paterna del Madera — circular desde Alcaraz
description: This difficult 42.2-mile (67.8 km) road cycling route from Alcaraz offers stunning views and challenging climbs through the Sierra de Alcara
region_intro: Road cycling around Alcaraz offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, wooded areas, and significant elevation changes. The region features a network of roads that traverse varied terrain, including open plains and more challenging mountain passes. Road cyclists can expect routes that navigate through natural parks and past historical towns, providing a mix of scenic views and physical demands. Best road cycling routes around Alcaraz The most popular road cycling route is Alcaraz — circular from Riópar Viejo, a 54.4 miles (87.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route features substantial elevation gain through the Alcaraz mountains. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Circular El Barrancazo y Paterna del Madera, a difficult 42.1 miles (67.8 km) path. This route explores ravines and wooded areas, offering a challenging ride with scenic views. Local road cyclists also love the Vilanueva de la Fuente, Viveros y Robledo — circular desde Alcaraz, a 39.8 miles (64.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including nurseries and oak forests, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Road cycling around Alcaraz is defined by its rolling hills, wooded valleys, and challenging mountain passes, with routes available for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Alcaraz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from 7 reviews. More than 1,300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcaraz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Alcaraz?
A: Alcaraz offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 60 different tours available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to very challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Alcaraz?
A: Road cycling in Alcaraz is characterized by rolling hills, wooded valleys, and challenging mountain passes. The region features a network of roads that traverse varied terrain, including open plains and significant elevation changes through natural parks and past historical towns.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Alcaraz?
A: While many routes in Alcaraz are challenging, there are a few easier options available. For instance, you can find 2 routes specifically categorized as easy, offering a gentler introduction to the region's landscapes.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Alcaraz?
A: Experienced road cyclists will find plenty of demanding routes in Alcaraz, with 43 tours categorized as difficult. Notable challenging options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167476063/alcaraz-circular-from-riopar-viejo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaraz — circular from Riópar Viejo&lt;/a&gt;, a 87.5 km route with substantial elevation gain, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167499328/circular-el-barrancazo-y-paterna-del-madera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular El Barrancazo y Paterna del Madera&lt;/a&gt;, which explores ravines and wooded areas over 67.8 km.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Alcaraz?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Alcaraz are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167476063/alcaraz-circular-from-riopar-viejo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaraz — circular from Riópar Viejo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1335022614/vilanueva-de-la-fuente-viveros-y-robledo-circular-desde-alcaraz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vilanueva de la Fuente, Viveros y Robledo — circular desde Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along road cycling routes in Alcaraz?
A: The region around Alcaraz is rich in natural beauty. Cyclists can encounter stunning sights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Mundo River&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5318691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batán Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. There are also impressive viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3484172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Landscapes Around Riópar&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3933686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenara Peak&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or towns to explore near the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Road cycling routes often pass through or near historical sites. You can explore the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4080509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt; itself, including its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5919080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor and Towers&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable historical points include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5588608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riópar Viejo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4577208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Yedra Ruins&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Alcaraz?
A: The best time for road cycling in Alcaraz generally spans from spring through autumn. Summers can be warm, especially in open areas, but the higher elevations offer cooler temperatures. Spring and autumn provide pleasant weather conditions for longer rides.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Alcaraz?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation, there are some easier options that could be suitable for families with older children or those looking for a less strenuous ride. It's best to check the difficulty ratings and elevation profiles for specific routes to ensure they match your family's ability.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Alcaraz?
A: The road cycling routes in Alcaraz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from rolling hills to challenging mountain passes, and the opportunity to cycle through natural parks and past historical towns.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling trails in Alcaraz?
A: Parking availability varies by route and starting point. Many routes begin in or near towns like Alcaraz, Riópar Viejo, or Vianos, where public parking is typically available. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for recommended starting points and parking information.

Q: Are there any hidden gem road cycling routes in Alcaraz?
A: Beyond the most popular routes, Alcaraz offers many less-trafficked roads that provide a sense of discovery. Exploring the numerous moderate and difficult routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31845696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Crucetillas – Puerto de las Crucetillas loop from Vianos&lt;/a&gt;, can lead to stunning views and a more secluded cycling experience away from the main thoroughfares.
tips: A remote town in the Alcaraz mountain range, it is located in a protected natural area. Good place if you need water - it has a drinking fountain and a supermarket.

Without a doubt, the Sanctuary of Cortes is the most visited place in the province of Albacete.
In 1976, an article by W. A. Christian on Spanish sanctuaries had been published; in it he made a classification of the importance of these centers of popular devotion, today also called local, based on the geographical area occupied by the devotees who went to each one of them; established four national sanctuaries and a dozen regional ones; Among the latter was that of the Virgin of Cortes de Alcaraz, this being the only one in the Castilian-La Mancha region that was considered to be of this category. From this, without a doubt, it was inferred that the Cortes was a very significant sanctuary, and today it maintains that character.

The walk through the town offers you innumerable details, ornaments, arches, doorways and balconies. The Customs gate, the Galiano house, the Nuestra Señora de las Cortes sanctuary, the Santa María Magdalena monastery, the San Francisco convent or the Grogojí tower are some of the treasures hidden among its streets and landscapes this beautiful town from Albacete.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Peñascosa y Paterna del Madera — circular desde Alcaraz route?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a difficult road cycling route, requiring very good fitness. It features a significant accumulated ascent of around 1,300 meters over approximately 68 kilometers, with two distinct and substantial climbing segments. It is not suitable for beginners.

faqs.question: What can I expect to see along this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route offers beautiful views throughout the Sierra de Alcaraz. You'll pass through picturesque towns like Paterna del Madera and Alcaraz. You'll also catch glimpses of the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Cortes, follow the banks of the River Cortes, and cross the River Arquillo, enjoying scenic landscapes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route?
faqs.answer: Since the route starts and ends in Alcaraz, you should look for parking options within the town. As it's a circular route, you will return to your starting point.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for families or children?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and requires very good fitness due to its significant elevation gain and length. It is not recommended for families with children or beginner cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the significant elevation and location in the Sierra de Alcaraz, spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle this route. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring cold temperatures or even snow at higher elevations, making conditions challenging.

faqs.question: Are there any places to rest or refuel along the way?
faqs.answer: Paterna del Madera serves as a natural halfway point on the route, offering an opportunity to rest and potentially refuel before continuing the second half of the challenging ride.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the [CIMA AB02] El Barrancazo, [CIMA AB06] Loma de la Albarda, Sendero de los Serranos, and Camino Histórico Paterna-Riópar, among others.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions against dogs on public roads, this is a demanding road cycling route. If you choose to bring a dog, ensure it is safely secured and able to keep pace, or consider alternative, less strenuous routes more suitable for pets.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Peñascosa y Paterna del Madera — circular desde Alcaraz route, as it primarily uses public roads.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Komoot estimates the pure riding time for this 68-kilometer route to be around 3 hours and 35 minutes. However, given its 'difficult' classification and significant climbs, plan for a longer overall duration, including stops for rest, hydration, and enjoying the views.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is entirely on paved roads, suitable for a racebike. However, the terrain is characterized by a varied and demanding profile with long, sustained climbs and descents through the mountainous Sierra de Alcaraz.
